data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement the inference-recall replay measurement that validates the inference-challenge system by replaying the curator's incident corpus (from S1) and measuring recall and precision against the SC4 thresholds.

**Why**: SC4 requires in-session validation that the deployed inference-challenge system would have caught historical inference failures. The replay gate provides the closure criterion before the epic can close.

**Scope**:
- IN: Replay mechanism that processes incidents.jsonl (corpus from S1) through the inference-challenge system; recall measurement against all incidents (pass: >=50%); recall measurement against held-out partition from holdout.txt (pass: >=70%); precision measurement (pass: >=0.5); CORPUS_INSUFFICIENT signal emission when corpus has <10 incidents; pass/fail gate as in-session closure gate (not post-deploy); replay must invoke the exact affects_fields classifier shipped in S3 (not a reimplementation or stub)
- OUT: Curator agent definition and corpus creation (S1); dogfooding validation (S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, replay against incidents.jsonl shows inference challenge recall >= 50% of all incidents
  ← Satisfies: "replay shows challenges would have surfaced >=50% of all incidents" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, recall >= 70% specifically against the held-out partition in holdout.txt
  ← Satisfies: ">=70% of held-out partition specifically" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, precision (fired challenges mapping to real incidents / total fired) >= 0.5
  ← Satisfies: "precision... >=0.5" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, when the corpus has <10 incidents, CORPUS_INSUFFICIENT signal is emitted and no precision/recall gates are evaluated
  ← Satisfies: "If first run produces fewer than 10 incidents, SC4 escalates with CORPUS_INSUFFICIENT signal" (SC4)
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Recall measurements depend on corpus quality from S1 — a low-quality corpus (misclassified incidents, unverified ticket-ids) will produce spurious recall failures; implementation plan should include a corpus quality check before replay
- [Performance] Replay against the full corpus needs bounded runtime for reasonable CI execution — batch size and timeout strategy required
- [Testing] Replay must invoke the exact affects_fields classifier from S3, not a reimplementation or stub; if S4 is implemented before S3 lands, a stub may be used during development but the final closure gate must use the production classifier (adversarial review finding — implicit shared state with S3)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a planning infrastructure maintainer, inference-recall replay measurement validates that the challenge system would have surfaced historical inference failures at required recall and precision thresholds
